heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Exmouth
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Exmouth? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Exmouth so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Exmouth is the doorway to Ningaloo Reef, where kids snorkel over coral straight off the beach and, in season, swim beside whale sharks. Accommodation is limited and the season is short, so the park you choose and when you book matter more here than almost anywhere.
local_knowledge: Turquoise Bay's drift snorkel is the family highlight: walk up the beach, float down over the coral, walk back and repeat. Confident swimmers aged 8 and up handle it well on calm days. Whale shark season runs roughly March to July and tours take kids, though the price makes it a splurge. RAC Exmouth Cape is the facilities pick in town with two warm pools and nightly movies. Bullara Station, an hour south, is a different holiday altogether: farm animals, damper nights and outback community, and families consistently rate it the trip highlight. Everything from April to October books out far ahead, fuel and groceries cost more here, and Cape Range's gorges are worth a non-beach day.

if_we_were_booking.label: Facilities in town
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose RAC Exmouth Cape Holiday Park for the two warm resort-style pools, games room and nightly movies, with town's shops and tour departures minutes away.

if_we_were_booking.label: The outback station experience
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Bullara Station Stay, an hour south, for farm animals, campfire damper and the kind of station atmosphere kids remember for years. No pool, and it doesn't matter.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Split the trip. A few nights at Bullara on the way in, then the rest in Exmouth for the reef. You get two completely different holidays, and the contrast is the magic.

faq.question: Is Exmouth good for kids?
faq.answer: For water-loving families it's one of the best trips in Australia. Snorkelling straight off the beach at Turquoise Bay, turtles, and whale sharks in season. It suits school-aged kids best.

faq.question: What age can kids snorkel Turquoise Bay?
faq.answer: Confident swimmers from around 8 handle the drift snorkel on calm days. Younger kids can paddle the sheltered bay end. Always check conditions, the current is the whole point of the drift.

faq.question: When is whale shark season?
faq.answer: Roughly March to July. Tours run daily in season and take children, though minimum ages and prices vary by operator.

faq.question: Which Exmouth park is best for families?
faq.answer: RAC Exmouth Cape has the best facilities in town, with two warm pools, a games room and nightly movies. Bullara Station, an hour south, is the memorable outback alternative.

faq.question: What is Bullara Station like with kids?
faq.answer: A working station with animals, open space, campfire nights and a strong community feel. No pool and limited playground, and families rate it a trip highlight anyway.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: For April to October, months ahead at minimum. Exmouth's beds are limited and the season is short, so accommodation is the first thing to lock in.

faq.question: Is Coral Bay or Exmouth better for families?
faq.answer: Both sit on Ningaloo. Exmouth has more accommodation, services and Cape Range access; Coral Bay is smaller with reef right off the main beach. Many families do both.

faq.question: What should families do away from the water?
faq.answer: Cape Range National Park's gorges, the Yardie Creek boat tour, the lighthouse lookout for whales in season, and Exmouth's playgrounds and skatepark in town.

faq.question: Is the water safe for young children?
faq.answer: The sheltered corners at Turquoise Bay and town beach are calm in good weather. Watch current advice at the drift sections and swim within the sanctuary zones.

faq.question: How remote is Exmouth really?
faq.answer: It's a 13-hour drive from Perth, groceries and fuel cost more, and phone coverage fades in the national park. Stock up and plan like it's remote, because it is.
